---
title: TTL
description: Time-to-live format — string shortcuts and millisecond numbers.
icon: Timer
---

## Format

TTL values can be expressed as either a **string** (human-readable shorthand) or a **number** (milliseconds).

### String format

```ts
await cache.set("key", value, { ttl: "5m" });
```

| Unit | Suffix | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Milliseconds | `ms` | `"500ms"` |
| Seconds | `s` | `"30s"` |
| Minutes | `m` | `"5m"` |
| Hours | `h` | `"1h"` |
| Days | `d` | `"7d"` |

Combinations are supported: `"1h30m"`, `"2d12h"`.

### Number format

Pass the duration in **milliseconds**:

```ts
await cache.set("key", value, { ttl: 60_000 }); // 1 minute
```

## parseTtl

Convert a TTL string to milliseconds using the exported `parseTtl` utility:

```ts
import { parseTtl } from "@justwant/cache";

parseTtl("5m");    // → 300000
parseTtl("1h30m"); // → 5400000
parseTtl(60_000);  // → 60000 (pass-through)
```

## Default TTL

Set a default TTL at cache creation time. Individual `set` / `wrap` calls override it:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createMemoryAdapter(),
  defaults: { ttl: "1h" },
});

// Uses default TTL
await cache.set("a", 1);

// Overrides with 5 minutes
await cache.set("b", 2, { ttl: "5m" });
```

## No expiration

Omit `ttl` and do not set a default to store a value permanently (until explicitly deleted):

```ts
await cache.set("config:feature-x", enabled);
```
